slides.title: Reusable Containers 📦
slides.content:
Functions aren't the only things that drastically benefit from Generics. You can radically supercharge Interfaces as well!

Imagine an API response object that always has a generic `status: number`, but the `data` payload can be a `User`, a `Post`, or a `Comment` depending on the request.

slides.title: The Generic Interface
slides.content: We add `<T>` right after the interface name, and then drop `T` in dynamically where the payload goes.

quizData.question: Based on the interface above, what would be the comprehensive type of a variable storing a fully successful API response containing a literal Array of numbers `[1, 2, 3]` inside its data payload?
quizData.explanation: You dynamically pass the exact intended Type (`number[]`) into the `<T>` slot of the Interface generic.

slides.title: Real World Use Case 🌍
slides.content: In actual production apps, Generics wrap custom data models seamlessly.

slides.title: Standard Practice 💎
slides.content: Notice how `Array<string>` from Chapter 5 makes total sense now! An Array is natively just a profoundly massive Generic Interface!
